evidences.title: Fetching 1 minute bars from Yahoo Finance - Stack Overflow
evidences.content: RasikhJ's user avatar ## 1 Comment Add a comment Yes there is other APIs. I don't know if it can still help but if you need intraday data, there is a API on rapidapi called (Quotient) which allows to pull intraday (at 1-min level), EOD market (FX, Crypto, Stocks (US, CANADIAN, UK, AUSTRALIA, EUROPE), ETFs and Futures. It also provides earnings, dividends, splits and a lot others informations. belquant009's user avatar ## Comments Add a comment Yes, you can use Interactive Broker's API. This is consistent and accurate source of data, support over many markets and instruments. In order to work with IB data, you need to install IB TWS or IB Gateway. Here is a code snippet of Forex "EURUSD" Data on minutes level. [...] Thanks! Danny's user avatar ## 3 Answers 3 Historical minute data is not as easily accessible as we all would like. I have found that the most affordable way to gather Intraday Stock Price data is to develop automated scripts that log price information for whenever the markets are open. evidences.title: Meta Stock Split 2026: Will META Finally Pull the Trigger?
evidences.content: Recent price data show Meta Platforms in a consolidation phase after a huge two-year rally. Latest close (12 December 2025): about $644.23 per share. After-hours quote: around $643.56 per share. 52-week range: roughly $479–796, with an all-time high near $789–796 in mid-August 2025. Market capitalisation: about $1.6–1.7 trillion at current prices. Meta remains one of the best-performing "Magnificent Seven" names since the 2022–23 tech wash-out, with the share price more than doubling from late-2023 levels as digital advertising recovered and AI-related revenue opportunities scaled up. ## Meta Fundamentals Going into 2026 Under the hood, the business is still growing hard. evidences.title: Will Meta Platforms Enact a Stock Split in 2025? - Yahoo Finance
evidences.content: One company that has never split its stock but may consider doing so in 2025 is Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META), formerly known as Facebook. Meta went public as Facebook in 2012, trading around $38 per share. Now, the stock trades for nearly $600, which could be a threshold at which it starts thinking about splitting its stock. Start Your Mornings Smarter! Wake up with Breakfast news in your inbox every market day. Sign Up For Free » ## Meta's stock price is near the point at which its peers split If we're speculating if Meta will split its stock, we need to look at when some of its peers have chosen to do so. The obvious comparisons are with its fellow "Magnificent Seven" stocks: Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Nvidia, and Tesla. evidences.title: Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) Valuation Measures & Financial Statistics
evidences.content: Meta Platforms Inc's valuation metrics have improved, with the P/E ratio decreasing to 27.52x in Q4 FY 2025 from 31.50x in Q3 FY 2025, indicating a more attractive pricing for investors. Profitability metrics have shown a decline, with operating margin decreasing to 41.44% in Q4 FY 2025 from 43.23% in Q3 FY 2025, suggesting challenges in maintaining operational efficiency. Financial strength remains solid, highlighted by a stable debt-to-equity ratio of 0.39 in Q4 FY 2025, up from 0.26 in Q3 FY 2025, indicating increased reliance on debt financing.
